name: Caleb
description: Caleb, son of Jephunneh, stands as a paragon of faith and courage in Jewish tradition. He was one of the twelve spies sent by Moses to scout the land of Canaan, and along with Joshua, he was one of only two who returned with a positive report, urging the Israelites to seize the land God had promised them. Unlike the other spies, who focused on the perceived insurmountable obstacles and the strength of the Canaanite inhabitants, Caleb and Joshua trusted in God's power to deliver them. This unwavering faith distinguished him from the rest of his generation, leading to the fulfillment of God's promise that he would inherit the land he had explored. Caleb's steadfastness earned him a place of honor among the Israelites. He played a crucial role in the conquest of Canaan under Joshua's leadership, specifically claiming Hebron as his inheritance, a city previously inhabited by giants. Even in his old age, Caleb displayed remarkable vigor and determination, personally leading the charge to drive out the Anakites from Hebron, demonstrating his unwavering commitment to God's commands and his enduring strength. Caleb's story serves as an enduring testament to the power of faith, courage, and obedience in the face of adversity, solidifying his position as a heroic figure in Jewish history and a role model for generations.
subtitle: Faithful spy and courageous leader.
achievements: One of two spies to give a positive report
Drove out the Anakites from Hebron

keyMyths.title: The Spies' Report
keyMyths.description: Caleb, along with Joshua, bravely dissented from the other spies, urging the Israelites to trust in God and conquer Canaan. Their faith set them apart and ensured their future inheritance.

keyMyths.title: Inheritance of Hebron
keyMyths.description: As a reward for his faithfulness, Caleb was granted the city of Hebron, a challenging territory inhabited by giants. He successfully conquered the city and established his claim.

keyMyths.title: Driving Out the Anakites
keyMyths.description: Even in his old age, Caleb demonstrated his strength and faith by personally leading the charge to drive out the formidable Anakites from Hebron. This act showcased his unwavering commitment.

sources.author: Moses
sources.description: Details Caleb's role as a spy and his positive report.
sources.text: Details Caleb's role as a spy and his positive report.

sources.author: Joshua
sources.description: Describes Caleb's inheritance and conquest of Hebron.
